data.title: B3 S.A. - Brasil, Bolsa, Balcão reports Q4 results
data.description:
• B3 S.A. - Brasil, Bolsa, Balcão press release (OTCPK:BOLSY): Q4 Net income was R$1.2 billion, an increase of 28.7% compared to 4Q23.
  • Revenue of R$2.67B (+7.2% Y/Y).

data.title: UK's flash manufacturing PMI hits 14-month low of 46.4 in February
New No. 1: Amazon takes the quarterly revenue crown from Walmart
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) has surpassed Walmart (NYSE:WMT) in quarterly sales for the first time ever, bringing the e-commerce giant closer to catching up with the world's largest retailer.

Walmart (NYSE:WMT) on Thursday reported fourth-quarter revenue of $180.55B, while Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) revenue for its fourth quarter was $187.79B. This is the first time that Amazon overtook Walmart, which was consistently the top quarterly revenue generator on Wall Street since 2012.

Still, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) is expected to lead in annual sales, with analysts on average expecting revenue of $703.02B for the current fiscal year, compared to the $699.19B consensus estimate for Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) revenue.

Amazon's (NASDAQ:AMZN) core retail business accounts for most of its revenue, but its cloud computing unit AWS and online advertising business have grown exponentially in recent years. Meanwhile, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) has been expanding its omnichannel strategy and its ad revenue continues to grow.

"This all-rounded approach - where retail is at the heart of the business, but other things generate revenue around it - is a model that's been really successful, and it's been an exemplar for other retailers to really copy Amazon in that strategy," Neil Saunders, managing director of GlobalData Retail, told Fortune.

Here's a comparison of the retailers' quarterly revenue in the last reported fiscal year:

data.title: Elekta AB reports Q3 results
data.description:

data.title: Sika AG reports FY results
data.description:
• Sika AG press release (OTCPK:SXYAY): FY GAAP EPS of CHF 7.76
  • Sika posts net sales of CHF 11,763.1 million (+4.7% in CHF) in 2024▪ Sales growth of 7.4% in local currencies▪ Increase in material margin to 54.5% (2023: 53.6%)▪ 11.0% growth in operating profit before depreciation and amortization (EBITDA) to CHF 2,269.5 million (previous year: CHF 2,044.7 million)▪ Strong increase in EBITDA margin to 19.3% (2023: 18.2%)▪ 17.4% jump in net profit to CHF 1,247.6 million (previous year: CHF 1,062.6million)▪ Proposed dividend per share of CHF 3.60 (previous year: CHF 3.30)
  • Outlook for fiscal 2025: Expected sales increase in local currencies of 3-6%, and over-proportional increase in EBITDA and rise in EBITDA margin to 19.5%-19.8%

data.title: Pacific Green sells Limestone Coast North to Intera
data.description:
• Pacific Green Technologies (OTCQB:PGTK) has signed an agreement to sell its Limestone Coast North Energy Park to Intera Renewables for A$460 million ($293 million).

  • The project, a 250MW / 500MWh battery energy storage development in South Australia, will begin operations in early 2027.

  • The project aims to strengthen the region's grid stability and support Australia's net-zero transition.

  • Intera is the renewable energy platform majority owned by funds managed by Palisade Investment Partners (Palisade), an Australian based infrastructure and real assets' manager.

  • As part of the transaction, Pacific Green will be retained to manage the construction of the project through to commercial operations, following which Palisade Integrated Management Services will carry out asset management activities through its role as Intera’s asset manager.

  • This project is part of Pacific Green's broader 10GWh development pipeline in Australia, which includes additional projects: Limestone Coast West (250MW / 1,000MWh), Portland Energy Park (1,000MW / 2,500MWh), and a new development in Wagga Wagga, NSW.

  • "The company has also secured a long-term tolling Power Purchase Agreement with Zen Energy", said Scott Poulter, Pacific Green’s CEO.

data.title: Europe markets slightly higher as investors read flash PMI numbers
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.01% to 8,662.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.02% to 22,316, Germany's flash PMI shows growth in service sector, while manufacturing struggles. Additionally, attention turned to upcoming elections on Sunday, with the center-right CDU leading the polls, while Chancellor Scholz's center-left SPD has fallen to third place.

France (CAC:IND) +0.33% to 8,149, France flash PMI readings shows worst decline in economic output since Sept 2023. France’s business climate indicator rose to 96 in February.

The consumer confidence indicator in the Czech Republic fell to 96.6 in February. The business confidence indicator in the Czech Republic increased to 98.

Euro Area services PMI at 3-month low in Feb, manufacturers cut purchasing activity amid weak demand.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.24% higher to 552.38, after a lackluster session the previous day.

In corporates, Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFF) shares surged after the British bank reported a decline in Q4 net profit but announced a $1.5 billion share buyback. Leonardo (DRS) also posted results that exceeded expectations.

Coming up in the next session: UK flash PMI readings expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down 1 basis point to 4.49%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 2.50%.

UK's 10-year yield was unchanged at 4.61%.

data.title: Genfit reports preliminary results of repurchase offer to 2025 OCEANEs holders
data.description:
Genfit S.A (NASDAQ:GNFT) Friday said that it has signed put option agreements with 2025 OCEANEs holders and the convening of the general meeting of the 2025 OCEANEs holders for March 10, 2025.

Genfit (NASDAQ:GNFT) has signed to date put option agreements with 2025 OCEANEs holders for a total of 1,812,475 2025 OCEANEs at a price of EUR 32.75 per bond, representing 95.3% of the outstanding 2025 OCEANEs.

2025 OCEANEs holders have until March 19, 2025 to exercise their option thereunder.
